markers.title: Brazil (#1)
markers.description: 2.6–3.8M tonnes annually · 38% global · Arabica + Robusta · 600–1,300m plateau

markers.title: Vietnam (#2)
markers.description: 1.5–1.96M tonnes · 17.7% global · Robusta · Central Highlands 500–800m volcanic basalt

markers.title: Colombia (#3)
markers.description: 760K tonnes · 7–8% global · 100% Arabica · Andean slopes 1,200–2,000m · 2 rainy seasons

markers.title: Indonesia (#4)
markers.description: 668K tonnes · 6.8% global · Robusta + Arabica · volcanic island geography

markers.title: Ethiopia (#5) — the birthplace
markers.description: 421K tonnes · 4.2% global · Arabica · coffee evolved naturally here; irreplaceable wild genetic diversity

markers.title: Yemen — Al-Mukha (historical)
markers.description: Original trade port (1450s Sufi use); gave the world the word 'mocha'

markers.title: London (historical)
markers.description: First coffeehouse 1652 · Charles II's 11-day ban 1675 · Lloyd's + Jonathan's born here

lines.title: Yemen → Ottoman trade route (15th–16th c.)
lines.description: Al-Mukha port to Constantinople — coffee's first major trade expansion

lines.title: Ottoman → Europe (17th c.)
lines.description: Constantinople → Venice → London — coffee reaches Europe via Mediterranean commerce

lines.title: Colonial Atlantic route (17th–19th c.)
lines.description: Ethiopia → Yemen → Saint-Domingue (Haiti) → Brazil — the plantation expansion fueled by enslaved labor

polygons.title: Coffee Belt (northern half)
polygons.description: Between 25°N and the equator — commercial coffee zone

polygons.title: Coffee Belt (southern half)
polygons.description: Between equator and 30°S — commercial coffee zone
